_notes.model_size: Int8-quantized ONNX (model_quantized.onnx) is ~42 MB. General-purpose dichotomous segmentation (schirrmacher/ormbg, packaged by onnx-community as a transformers.js-compatible RMBG-1.4 alternative). Replaces MODNet (which was portrait-optimized).
_notes.ort_version: Tied to onnxruntime-web 1.26.0-dev.20260416-b7804b056c (the version pinned by @huggingface/transformers 4.2.0). If transformers.js bumps, regenerate these wasm sha256 values.
_notes.pipeline: Loadable via pipeline('image-segmentation', 'bg-remove', { dtype: 'q8' }). model_type 'isnet' is registered for image-segmentation in v4.2.0's CUSTOM_ARCHITECTURES_MAPPING (verified in node_modules/@huggingface/transformers/src/models/registry.js:649). The model directory name 'bg-remove' is hardcoded in scripts/copy-bg-models.mjs and src/engines/image-bg-remove/model-loader.ts (MODEL_ID); the manifest's 'model' field controls the cache directory name only.
_notes.selection_rationale: briaai/RMBG-1.4 q8 (the spec's lead candidate) is unloadable in transformers.js 4.2.0 — see docs/superpowers/plans/phase-18-verification-log.md post-mortem. ormbg-ONNX is the only canonical-publisher port that combines (a) general-purpose RMBG-class architecture, (b) q8 weights at ~40 MB target size, (c) transformers.js-compatible model_type, and (d) Apache-2.0 license.
